title: Published crate and active package line
goal: Retrieve current release status and avoid falling back to stale rename-era wording.

records.text: femind is the active crate and repository. The earlier mindcore crate remains the legacy package line, while new work and future releases target femind.
Version 0.2.0 was released on 2026-03-26 with the unified HTTP MCP endpoint, the tracked documentation databases, and the vault migration fix for legacy plaintext rows.

retrieval_checks.query: Which crate line should new work target now?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: New work should target femind.

retrieval_checks.query: What happened to mindcore?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: mindcore remains the legacy package line.

retrieval_checks.query: What version was released on 2026-03-26?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: Version 0.2.0 was released on 2026-03-26.

abstention_checks.query: What exact date was mindcore first published to crates.io?

title: Cognitive memory types and lifecycle
goal: Verify retrieval across the intended cognitive memory model rather than flattened semantic-only memories.

records.text: The optimized memory model uses three cognitive memory types: episodic for events and sessions, semantic for durable facts and preferences, and procedural for workflows and how-to knowledge.
Memories move through working, long-term, and archive tiers based on importance and reuse instead of remaining in one flat bucket forever.
Evaluation should preserve episodic, semantic, and procedural distinctions so retrieval reflects the architecture rather than a simplified all-semantic test harness.

retrieval_checks.query: What are the three cognitive memory types in femind?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: The three cognitive memory types are episodic, semantic, and procedural.

retrieval_checks.query: What is procedural memory used for?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: Procedural memory is used for workflows and how-to knowledge.

retrieval_checks.query: Does femind use one flat memory bucket?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: No, memories move through working, long-term, and archive tiers.

abstention_checks.query: What fourth cognitive memory type is reserved for emotions?

title: Vector search modes and runtime behavior
goal: Verify exact, ann, and off mode behavior for natural-language retrieval questions.

records.text: Vector search modes: exact uses brute-force cosine similarity by default, ann uses HNSW via instant-distance with a shared in-memory index that rebuilds when stored vectors change, and off uses FTS5 keyword search only.
femind combines FTS5 keyword search, vector similarity, and hybrid retrieval with reciprocal rank fusion.

retrieval_checks.query: What does exact mode use in femind?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: Exact mode uses brute-force cosine similarity.

retrieval_checks.query: What does ann mode use?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: ann mode uses HNSW via instant-distance with a shared in-memory index.

retrieval_checks.query: What happens when vector search mode is off?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: off uses FTS5 keyword search only.

retrieval_checks.query: Does ann mode require a remote vector database?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: No. ann uses a shared in-memory index.

title: Provider defaults and fallbacks
goal: Retrieve the locked extraction defaults without mixing API and CLI lanes.

records.text: Recommended API extraction default: DeepInfra openai/gpt-oss-120b.
Recommended CLI extraction default: Codex CLI gpt-5.4-mini. Lower-cost CLI fallback: Codex CLI gpt-5.1-codex-mini.
Use all-MiniLM-L6-v2 as the default embedding model on all targets because it compiles to WASM, runs faster on CPU, and is available on DeepInfra and other API providers.

retrieval_checks.query: What is the default API extraction model for femind?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: The default API extraction model is openai/gpt-oss-120b.

retrieval_checks.query: What is the default CLI extraction model?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: The default CLI extraction model is gpt-5.4-mini.

retrieval_checks.query: What is the lower-cost CLI fallback?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: The lower-cost CLI fallback is gpt-5.1-codex-mini.

retrieval_checks.query: What is the default embedding model?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: The default embedding model is all-MiniLM-L6-v2.

retrieval_checks.query: Is Llama 3.3 the locked default extraction model for femind?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: No. gpt-oss-120b is the locked API default.

title: Validation order and benchmark position
goal: Make sure larger benchmark work stays secondary to practical real-world validation.

records.text: Validation order: practical eval scenarios first, then a small approved live validation pass, then fixes for real-world failures, then repeatable local regression checks, and only then secondary benchmark comparison if it is still useful.
Benchmark datasets are secondary and should only be used after the practical eval set is directionally strong.

retrieval_checks.query: What should happen before benchmark comparisons?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: Practical eval scenarios and real-world fixes should happen before benchmark comparisons.

retrieval_checks.query: Are benchmark datasets the primary design target?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: No, benchmark datasets are secondary.

retrieval_checks.query: Should LongMemEval be the first gating step before any practical evaluation?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: No. Practical evaluation comes first and benchmarks are secondary.

title: Current practical and live-usage baselines
goal: Retrieve the validated baselines without collapsing practical and larger-sample runs together.

records.text: The current practical baseline is: extraction-only practical eval with openai/gpt-oss-120b passes 4/4, Codex CLI gpt-5.4-mini passes 4/4, Codex CLI gpt-5.1-codex-mini passes 4/4, retrieval-only exact passes 9/9, and retrieval-only ann passes 9/9.
The broader live-usage sample from actual project docs passes 11/11 across all four tested extraction models.

retrieval_checks.query: How is the practical retrieval exact baseline doing?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: The practical retrieval exact baseline passes 9/9.

retrieval_checks.query: How is ann doing on the practical set?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: The practical retrieval ann baseline passes 9/9.

retrieval_checks.query: How did the broader live-usage sample perform?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: The broader live-usage sample passes 11/11 across the four tested extraction models.

abstention_checks.query: What was the exact prompt-token cost of the 11/11 live-usage run?

title: Standard maintainer commands
goal: Test procedural retrieval for the commands maintainers should actually run.

records.text: The primary live-validation entry point is scripts/run-practical-eval.sh.

The standard local live-validation path uses retrieval mode, exact vector mode, the api extraction backend, openai/gpt-oss-120b, and writes a summary artifact to target/practical-eval/retrieval-exact.json.
Non-LLM verification should include cargo test, cargo test --features full, and cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings.

retrieval_checks.query: What script should a maintainer run for the standard live validation path?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: A maintainer should run scripts/run-practical-eval.sh.

retrieval_checks.query: What vector mode is used in the standard local live-validation path?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: The standard local path uses exact vector mode.

retrieval_checks.query: What are the standard non-LLM verification commands?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: The standard non-LLM verification commands are cargo test, cargo test --features full, and cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings.

retrieval_checks.query: Is the standard validation entry point scripts/run-benchmark-suite.sh?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: No. The primary live-validation entry point is scripts/run-practical-eval.sh.

title: Documentation source of truth
goal: Verify that the repo-local documentation database and markdown docs are retrieved coherently.

records.text: The primary local source of truth is .docs/femind_spec.db. The markdown docs in the repo should stay aligned with that database.
The key maintainer documents are ARCHITECTURE.md, RESEARCH.md, DECISIONS.md, PRACTICAL_EVAL.md, and the eval/practical directory.

retrieval_checks.query: What is the primary local source of truth for femind docs?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: The primary local source of truth is .docs/femind_spec.db.

retrieval_checks.query: Should markdown docs diverge from the spec database?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: No, the markdown docs should stay aligned with the spec database.

retrieval_checks.query: Is the primary local source of truth file femind_architecture.sql?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: No. The primary local source of truth is .docs/femind_spec.db.

title: Migration and legacy compatibility
goal: Verify the system can answer current-vs-legacy package questions without confusing the active line.

records.text: femind is the successor to the earlier mindcore crate and repository.
Legacy compatibility hooks remain so older .mindcore databases and mindcore_meta tables can still be opened safely.
The published mindcore crate remains the legacy package line; new work and future releases should target femind.

retrieval_checks.query: What is femind the successor to?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: femind is the successor to the earlier mindcore crate and repository.

retrieval_checks.query: Why do mindcore references still exist in some storage code?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: Legacy compatibility hooks remain so older .mindcore databases and mindcore_meta tables can still be opened safely.

retrieval_checks.query: Should new work still target the mindcore crate name?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: No. New work and future releases should target femind.

title: Messy maintainer note extraction
goal: Stress extraction on rough maintainer notes rather than clean prose.

records.text: notes from today: exact/ann both look good on practical set, ann not obviously worse. don't chase LongMemEval yet. lock gpt-oss-120b for API extraction, gpt-5.4-mini for CLI, keep 5.1 codex mini as cheap fallback. need bigger real-world library next, not another benchmark rabbit hole.

extraction_checks.expected_fact: gpt-oss-120b is the locked API extraction default.
gpt-5.4-mini is the locked CLI extraction default.
gpt-5.1-codex-mini should remain the low-cost CLI fallback.
A bigger real-world library should come before more benchmark work.

title: Current versus superseded plan
goal: Check yes-no current-state reasoning against superseded plans.

records.text: Early thought: benchmark sweeps should drive the next phase before wider live validation.
Current accepted plan: larger real-world validation should come before broader benchmark sweeps, and benchmarks remain secondary unless they are needed for regression.

retrieval_checks.query: Should broader benchmark sweeps drive the next phase right now?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: No. Larger real-world validation should come before broader benchmark sweeps.

retrieval_checks.query: What is the current accepted plan for follow-up testing?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: The current accepted plan is to do larger real-world validation before broader benchmark sweeps.

title: Provider comparison results
goal: Retrieve real comparison outcomes from the current approved provider set.

records.text: The broader live-usage sample passes 11/11 across four tested extraction models: openai/gpt-oss-120b, zai-org/GLM-4.7-Flash, gpt-5.4-mini, and gpt-5.1-codex-mini.
The preferred defaults are gpt-oss-120b for the API lane, gpt-5.4-mini for the CLI lane, and gpt-5.1-codex-mini as the lower-cost CLI fallback.

retrieval_checks.query: How many extraction models passed the broader live-usage sample?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: Four extraction models passed the broader live-usage sample.

retrieval_checks.query: Which API model is the preferred default after comparison?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: gpt-oss-120b is the preferred API default.

retrieval_checks.query: Which CLI model is the preferred default after comparison?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: gpt-5.4-mini is the preferred CLI default.

abstention_checks.query: Did Nemotron fail the broader live-usage sample and get removed from the candidate set?

title: Large library runner and artifact paths
goal: Stress exact path and runner-name retrieval for the larger real-world library.

records.text: The larger real-world validation library is run with scripts/run-live-library.sh.
The standard live-library run uses all mode, exact vector mode, the api extraction backend, openai/gpt-oss-120b, and writes its summary to target/live-library/live-library-exact.json.

retrieval_checks.query: What script runs the larger real-world library?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: The larger real-world library is run with scripts/run-live-library.sh.

retrieval_checks.query: What summary file does the standard live-library run write?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: The standard live-library run writes to target/live-library/live-library-exact.json.

retrieval_checks.query: What vector mode does the standard live-library run use?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: The standard live-library run uses exact vector mode.

retrieval_checks.query: Does the standard live-library run write to target/live-library/live-library-ann.json by default?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: No. The standard live-library run writes to target/live-library/live-library-exact.json by default.

title: Provider cost tracking limitations
goal: Verify the system can state what the current run summaries capture and what they do not.

records.text: Live-library summaries include scenario path, scenario count, retrieval mode, vector mode, embedding model, extraction backend, extraction model, duration, passed checks, total checks, and pass rate.
The current runner does not capture provider token-usage telemetry yet, so cost should be tracked as a provider-price snapshot rather than as an exact per-run bill.

retrieval_checks.query: What does the current live-library summary record about a run?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: The current live-library summary records mode, vector mode, models, duration, and pass counts.

retrieval_checks.query: Does the current runner capture exact provider token-usage telemetry?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: No. The current runner does not capture exact provider token-usage telemetry yet.

retrieval_checks.query: How should cost be tracked for now?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: Cost should be tracked as a provider-price snapshot rather than as an exact per-run bill.

abstention_checks.query: What exact dollar cost did the last live-library run incur?

title: Feature flag boundaries and exclusions
goal: Test combined-capability negatives and exact feature-boundary questions.

records.text: Compile-time feature flags include api-embeddings for the DeepInfra embedding API, api-llm for the chat completions API, ann for HNSW approximate nearest neighbor, and full for everything except encryption and mcp-server.

retrieval_checks.query: What does the full feature set include?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: The full feature set includes everything except encryption and mcp-server.

retrieval_checks.query: Does the full feature set include encryption and mcp-server together?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: The full feature set includes everything except encryption and mcp-server.

abstention_checks.query: What exact feature flag enables only SQLCipher encryption?

title: Stale versus current large-library baseline
goal: Make sure the system prefers the current large-library result over an older intermediate note.

records.text: Intermediate note: the larger real-world library currently passes 36/44 in both all+exact and all+ann, with misses concentrated in abstention behavior.
Current larger real-world baseline: the live-library corpus now passes 44/44 in both all+exact and all+ann.

retrieval_checks.query: What is the current large-library baseline for exact and ann?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: The current large-library baseline is 44/44 in both all+exact and all+ann.

retrieval_checks.query: Is 36/44 still the current large-library baseline?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: No. The current large-library baseline is 44/44.

abstention_checks.query: What exact minute was the 36/44 intermediate note recorded?

title: Rough note extraction for release and cost caveats
goal: Stress extraction on rough notes with mixed validation, release, and cost-tracking caveats.

records.text: scratchpad: 0.2.0 is already out, exact + ann both 44/44 on the big live library, don't write that we have true provider cost accounting yet because runner only tracks duration/pass counts for now, next real benchmark step is recallbench only after this bigger corpus stays stable.

extraction_checks.expected_fact: The large live-library corpus passes 44/44 in both exact and ann mode.
The current runner does not yet provide true provider cost accounting.
RecallBench should come only after the larger corpus remains stable.

title: Release date and version traps
goal: Exercise exact date/version retrieval and unsupported finer-grained detail questions.

records.text: Version 0.2.0 was released on 2026-03-26.
The earlier mindcore package line remains legacy only, while femind is the active crate line.

retrieval_checks.expected_answer: femind is the active crate line.
